intro: Codonoboea ridleyana, also known as the "Malaysian Snake Grass," is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its purported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Codonoboea ridleyana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
